result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a Bayesian Search Strategy using a Probabilistic Field Estimator and a Search Strategy Optimizer. The estimator maintains a belief map P(S) of oil locations by updating the probability of each cell being part of a polyomino shape based on drilling results and noisy aggregate queries using the formula P(S|Q, D) proportional to P(Q|S) * P(S|D). The optimizer selects the next action (drilling for high-certainty local verification or querying for global information gain) by maximizing the expected Bayesian information gain per unit cost. The process terminates when the uncertainty in the belief map is sufficiently low to allow a final guess with minimal expected error cost. JUSTIFICATION: This approach minimizes total probing cost by balancing the high cost of drilling with the lower-cost, noisy information provided by aggregate queries, using a greedy heuristic to maximize information gain per query cost.
